what_it_checks: hermes binary is resolvable on PATH
failure_symptom: All tool/subprocess calls to the host fail immediately
likely_causes: Hermes not installed
PATH not exported in this shell/agent env
fix_steps: Run: which hermes
If missing, install/repair Hermes Agent and ensure its bin dir is on PATH
For agents, confirm the spawned shell inherits PATH
change_robust: Uses shutil.which at runtime — independent of any hardcoded path
detection_method: shutil.which(ctx.hermes_bin) is not None

what_it_checks: hermes --version is parseable (host is a known-good build)
failure_symptom: Unknown host version; contract assumptions may not hold
likely_causes: Hermes build does not print the expected banner
Broken install
fix_steps: Run: hermes --version
Reinstall Hermes if the version banner is missing
change_robust: Parses live --version output, not a pinned constant
detection_method: regex 'Hermes Agent v<ver>' matches `hermes --version`

what_it_checks: host advertises -z/--oneshot (the non-interactive entry every plugin relies on)
failure_symptom: Plugin/CLI falls into interactive mode or errors when invoking the host
likely_causes: A Hermes update renamed/removed the oneshot flag
fix_steps: Run: hermes --help | grep -- -z
If absent, pin to a compatible Hermes version or update the plugin to the new flag
change_robust: Greps live --help output each run — detects a Hermes update that breaks the contract
detection_method: '-z' and '--oneshot' present in `hermes --help`

what_it_checks: this plugin's entry point is registered under hermes_agent.plugins and import+register() works
failure_symptom: Plugin not loaded by Hermes; its tools/subcommand are absent
likely_causes: pip install incomplete
entry point metadata missing
register() import error
fix_steps: Run: python -c "import importlib.metadata as m; print([e.value for e in m.entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') if 'cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder' in e.name.lower()])"
Reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder
Confirm the entry module imports and exposes a callable register
change_robust: Reads live importlib.metadata.entry_points(), not a config file
detection_method: entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') contains this dist and register is callable

what_it_checks: this plugin's toolset 'supercoder' is listed and enabled by `hermes tools list`
failure_symptom: Model cannot see this plugin's tools; --toolsets silently ignores it
likely_causes: Plugin failed to register its toolset
Hermes toolset registration API changed
fix_steps: Run: hermes tools list | grep supercoder
If missing, reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder and restart the host
Verify register_tool used a valid toolset name
change_robust: Queries live `hermes tools list` for 'supercoder' — catches contract drift on Hermes upgrade
detection_method: 'supercoder' appears under plugin toolsets in `hermes tools list`

what_it_checks: installed distribution version equals the package __version__
failure_symptom: Stale or mismatched install; fixes/behaviors may not be the deployed ones
likely_causes: Partial upgrade
multiple installs shadowing each other
fix_steps: Run: pip show cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder
Force a clean reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder
change_robust: Compares live importlib.metadata.version() to __version__ — no hardcoding
detection_method: importlib.metadata.version('cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder') == __version__

what_it_checks: native module supercoder_index_native imports (else pure-Python/subprocess fallback is used)
failure_symptom: Slower path; if fallback also broke, tool errors
likely_causes: wheel ABI/arch mismatch
native package not installed
fix_steps: Run: python -c 'import supercoder_index_native'
If ImportError, reinstall the bundled wheel; fallback keeps working meanwhile
change_robust: Attempts the import + hasattr at runtime; WARN (not fail) because fallback works
detection_method: import supercoder_index_native succeeds

what_it_checks: _wrap catches OSError/TypeError (IsADirectoryError, bad numeric args) → error JSON, never raises to host
failure_symptom: Traceback escapes into the Hermes host on a directory path or non-numeric arg
likely_causes: _wrap only caught (ValueError, FileNotFoundError, PermissionError)
fix_steps: Broaden _wrap except to (ValueError, TypeError, OSError)
Coerce payload ints defensively in runner.py
change_robust: Invokes a handler with a directory path / non-numeric arg and asserts error JSON
detection_method: handler raising IsADirectoryError/TypeError yields {ok:false,...}

what_it_checks: supercoder_repo_map core output is byte-identical across two identical calls (cache stats isolated under _stats)
failure_symptom: Two identical calls differ (outline_cache_hits 0 vs >0) — non-deterministic
likely_causes: observability counter mixed into the main payload
fix_steps: Move cache/observability counters under a _stats key or drop from payload
change_robust: Compares two consecutive call outputs for byte-equality
detection_method: two identical repo_map calls produce equal payloads (ignoring _stats)

what_it_checks: Whether the ruff linter binary can be found for the lint gate. Resolves in order: env override (CLUXION_SUPERCODER_RUFF_BIN), venv sibling, PATH.
failure_symptom: Lint gate returns checked=False with reason 'no_tool'. Python files pass patches without lint advisory findings.
likely_causes: ruff package installed in Python but binary not in venv/bin (Python-only wheel without CLI)
CLUXION_SUPERCODER_RUFF_BIN env var points to nonexistent path
ruff not in PATH and not in venv sibling location (venv/bin/ruff)
Wrong Python venv activated (ruff in wrong venv's bin/)
fix_steps: Verify ruff is installed: pip list | grep ruff (should be >=0.8 per pyproject.toml)
Check if binary exists: ls -la /path/to/venv/bin/ruff
If missing, reinstall ruff with pip install --force-reinstall 'ruff>=0.8'
Verify it works: which ruff or /path/to/venv/bin/ruff --version
If using env override, unset the var or point it to the correct binary: unset CLUXION_SUPERCODER_RUFF_BIN
change_robust: Call lint_gate.ruff_bin() which probes the actual resolution chain. Never hardcode PATH or venv location; the function adapts to env.
detection_method: Call: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.lint_gate import ruff_bin; print(ruff_bin())" — prints path if found, None if missing.

what_it_checks: Whether at least one of the three index backends (native, subprocess, python) can successfully scan a repo. The python tier always works as a fallback, but native and subprocess may fail if not installed/available.
failure_symptom: Repo map and cursor map operations fail with RuntimeError. Patch operations fail to detect file hashes. Index-heavy operations timeout or crash.
likely_causes: Native module present but corrupted or incompatible Rust code
Subprocess binary (supercoder-index) not in PATH and CLUXION_SUPERCODER_INDEX_BIN not set
Subprocess binary exists but crashes on certain file encodings or repo structures
Python tier encounters UnicodeDecodeError on non-UTF-8 files (python tier skips them gracefully, native may crash)
fix_steps: Test resolution: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.rust_bridge import resolve_backend; print(resolve_backend())"
Force python-only mode for debugging: export CLUXION_SUPERCODER_BACKEND=python
Test scan on a small, clean repo: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.rust_bridge import scan_repo; from pathlib import Path; print(scan_repo(Path('.'), max_files=5))"
If native is broken, try subprocess: export CLUXION_SUPERCODER_BACKEND=subprocess and verify the binary exists
Check for non-UTF-8 files in the repo that might crash native: find . -type f -exec file {} \; | grep -v UTF
If all else fails, use python-only: export CLUXION_SUPERCODER_BACKEND=python (slow but reliable)
change_robust: Detect via resolve_backend() function which polls all three tiers. Use env override to force a specific backend for testing. Probe with small test repo (max_files=1) to isolate the issue.
detection_method: Call: rust_bridge.scan_repo(Path('.'), max_files=1) — must return list with at least one entry or gracefully fall back to python tier. Check logs for backend resolution: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.rust_bridge import resolve_backend; print(resolve_backend())".

what_it_checks: Whether the supercoder-index subprocess binary is available if native module is not. This is the second-tier backend for repo scanning when the native Rust extension is unavailable.
failure_symptom: scan_repo falls back to python tier (slow). If native module is also missing, repo_map and repo scanning are slow but functional.
likely_causes: Rust binary not built or not installed in PATH
CLUXION_SUPERCODER_INDEX_BIN env var not set and binary not in project's rust/supercoder_index/target/release/
Binary exists but is for a different OS/architecture (e.g., linux binary on macos)
fix_steps: Check if binary exists in expected locations: ls -la /path/to/project/rust/supercoder_index/target/release/supercoder-index
If not, build it: cd rust/supercoder_index && cargo build --release
Verify it works: supercoder-index --help or /path/to/supercoder-index scan --help
If not in PATH, set env var: export CLUXION_SUPERCODER_INDEX_BIN=/full/path/to/supercoder-index
Confirm with: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.rust_bridge import resolve_backend; print(resolve_backend())" (should show 'subprocess' or 'native')
change_robust: Python tier is always available as fallback. Detect subprocess binary via shutil.which() and env var. Test resolution chain with resolve_backend().
detection_method: Call: shutil.which('supercoder-index') or check env CLUXION_SUPERCODER_INDEX_BIN. Also: python -c "import os; from pathlib import Path; configured = os.environ.get('CLUXION_SUPERCODER_INDEX_BIN', '').strip(); print('Binary at:', configured if configured else 'not set'); print('Exists:', Path(configured).exists() if configured else False)".

what_it_checks: Whether file_hash() function produces stable, deterministic SHA256 hashes across different reads and byte representations (CRLF normalization, UTF-8 consistency).
failure_symptom: Patch operations report hash mismatch (stale_file) even when the file content has not changed (false positives). Expected file hash and matched hash diverge for Windows line endings.
likely_causes: File read inconsistency: reading with different encodings or line-ending handling
Git line-ending conversions (core.safecrlf or auto-CRLF) alter file on disk between reads
hash_patch.py not normalizing CRLF to LF before hashing
Cursor created on one OS (e.g., Windows) used on another (Linux) with different line endings
fix_steps: Check file_hash implementation: grep -A5 'def file_hash' src/cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder/core/hash_patch.py — should call _normalize_newlines
Verify normalization: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.hash_patch import _normalize_newlines; print(repr(_normalize_newlines('a\\r\\nb')))" (should output 'a\\nb')
Check git line-ending config: git config core.autocrlf (should be false or input for stability)

Re-read file hash fresh: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.cursor import read_window; from pathlib import Path; w = read_window(Path('.'), 'file.py'); print('File hash:', w.file_hash)"
If mismatch persists, use expected_file_hash='' to force fuzzy match or re-read with read_window to get fresh hash
change_robust: Test file_hash() directly with various line ending styles. Verify _normalize_newlines is called in apply_patch(). Check that cursor.read_window() uses the same normalization.
detection_method: Read a file twice, normalize CRLF, and verify hashes match: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.hash_patch import file_hash; content = 'a=1\\r\\nb=2\\r\\n'; h1 = file_hash(content); h2 = file_hash(content.replace('\\r\\n', '\\n')); print('Match:', h1 == h2)".

what_it_checks: Whether the expected_file_hash cursor parameter is correctly capturing the file state at read time and is still valid when patch is applied.
failure_symptom: Patches apply to stale file content and modify the wrong code (silent data corruption). No stale_file error is reported when file changed between cursor creation and patch attempt.
likely_causes: expected_file_hash not passed to patch_tool (cursor not verified)
File modified by external process (another editor, git operation, build script) between read_window and patch_tool calls
Concurrent edits in multi-agent scenarios without cursor synchronization
Hash_patch.py not comparing expected_file_hash with current file hash
fix_steps: Always pass expected_file_hash from read_window result to patch_tool: patch_tool({..., 'expected_file_hash': window.file_hash, ...})
Before patching, re-read the file: read_window again to get fresh file_hash and verify no external changes
Check that apply_patch validates hash: grep -B2 -A5 'if expected_file_hash and current_hash' src/cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder/core/hash_patch.py
If file was modified externally, re-read and rebuild the patch from the current content
For multi-agent setups, use locks or cursors with timestamps to detect concurrent modifications
change_robust: Test by modifying file between read and patch. Verify apply_patch() calls _normalize_hash() and compares with current_hash. Ensure expected_file_hash parameter is mandatory in patch workflows.
detection_method: Read a file with read_window, record the file_hash, modify the file externally, then attempt patch with the old hash. Should fail with stale_file. Command: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.cursor import read_window; from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder import runner; from pathlib import Path; (Path('/tmp/t.py').write_text('x=1'); w = read_window(Path('.'), 't.py'); Path('/tmp/t.py').write_text('x=2'); r = runner.patch_tool({'cwd': '/tmp', 'path': 't.py', 'old_text': 'x=1', 'new_text': 'x=3', 'expected_file_hash': w.file_hash}); print('Blocked:', not r.ok))".

what_it_checks: Whether the syntax gate can parse the target language. Native and subprocess tiers support python/rust/js/ts/tsx/json via tree-sitter; python tier supports only python/json/toml via stdlib. Fail-open: unknown languages report checked=False and valid=True.
failure_symptom: Patches with broken syntax pass the gate when they should be reverted. Invalid Rust/JS/TS code applies without error. Syntax errors not caught, code compiles/runs incorrectly.
likely_causes: Python backend in use (CLUXION_SUPERCODER_BACKEND=python) but code is Rust/JS/TS — these languages not supported by python tier
Native module missing/broken — reverts to slower python tier which cannot parse compiled languages
Parser grammar for a language is outdated or missing in tree-sitter bindings
File encoding or content format incompatible with parser (e.g., minified JS)
fix_steps: Check backend in use: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.rust_bridge import resolve_backend; print(resolve_backend())"

Test syntax check directly: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.syntax_gate import check_source; r = check_source(content='<your code>', language='rust'); print('Checked:', r['checked'], 'Valid:', r['valid'])"
For Rust/JS/TS support, ensure native or subprocess backend is available; if not, syntax gate falls back to fail-open (no check) and patches always pass
Disable syntax_gate in patch_tool if false positives occur: patch_tool({..., 'syntax_gate': False, ...})
Check for encoding issues: file encoding must be UTF-8; non-UTF-8 files fail at read time before syntax gate is invoked
change_robust: Detect via check_source() call with known good/broken code. Verify backend supports target language. Test all three backends (native, subprocess, python) to understand fallback behavior.
detection_method: Call syntax_gate.check_source() with various languages and backends. Test python: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.syntax_gate import check_source; print(check_source(content='def f(): pass', language='python'))" — should have checked=True. Test rust with python backend: export CLUXION_SUPERCODER_BACKEND=python && python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.syntax_gate import check_source; r = check_source(content='fn f() {}', language='rust'); print('Checked:', r['checked'])" — should be False.

what_it_checks: Whether files in the workspace are valid UTF-8. patch_tool, read_window, and cursor_map all call .read_text(encoding='utf-8') which fails on non-UTF-8 files.
failure_symptom: patch_tool, read_window, cursor_map, and repo_map fail with UnicodeDecodeError. Operations on affected files are completely blocked.
likely_causes: Legacy files with Latin-1, CP1252, or other non-UTF-8 encodings
Binary files (images, compiled objects) with code extensions (.py, .rs, etc.) mixed in
Git check-in of files with wrong encoding
External tools generating non-UTF-8 output into repo
fix_steps: Identify non-UTF-8 files: file * or hexdump -C <file> | head to inspect byte sequences
Convert to UTF-8: iconv -f CP1252 -t UTF-8 <file> -o <file>.utf8 && mv <file>.utf8 <file> or python -c "open('file', 'rb').read().decode('latin-1').encode('utf-8')"
Exclude binaries from scanning: Check .gitignore and rust_bridge.SKIP_DIRS (node_modules, dist, target) are respected
If file cannot be converted, exclude it from the workspace or move it outside the repo root
Re-test: patch_tool and read_window should now succeed on UTF-8 files
change_robust: Detect via file command or Python's .read_text(encoding='utf-8'). Scan entire workspace to find all affected files. The error message includes the byte offset of the invalid sequence.
detection_method: Scan for non-UTF-8 files: find . -type f ! -name '*.bin' -exec sh -c 'file "$1" | grep -v UTF' _ {} \; or python -c "from pathlib import Path; [print(p) for p in Path('.').rglob('*') if p.is_file() and not str(p).startswith('.')]; import sys; for p in sys.argv[1:]: Path(p).read_text(encoding='utf-8')" /path/files.

what_it_checks: Whether repo_map honors the character budget contract: files beyond the budget are omitted but counted, never silently dropped. Truncated flag is set when omitted > 0.
failure_symptom: Model receives incomplete repo map without warning. Files are silently dropped, model doesn't know they exist. Truncated=False when files_omitted > 0 (contract violation).
likely_causes: repo_map.py logic skips files without incrementing files_omitted counter
Budget is not enforced consistently (some files counted, others not)
truncated flag set to False when files were actually dropped
Symbol capping doesn't match file capping logic (files included but symbols omitted inconsistently)
fix_steps: Check repo_map.py build_repo_map() logic: grep -A30 'def build_repo_map' and verify 'files_omitted' counter is incremented when budget is exhausted
Verify contract: python -c "assert result['files_mapped'] + result['files_omitted'] == result['files_scanned']"
Check truncated flag: if files_omitted > 0, truncated must be True; if files_omitted == 0, truncated must be False
Test with tiny budget: build_repo_map(repo, budget_chars=200) and verify at least one file is omitted
Read the map text to confirm visual consistency: print(result['map']) should show only included files
change_robust: Build repo map on real repo with various budgets and verify the formula: files_mapped + files_omitted == files_scanned. Check truncated flag in every build. The logic is deterministic; test with fixtures.
detection_method: Build repo map with small budget and verify files_mapped + files_omitted == files_scanned and truncated reflects overflow: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.repo_map import build_repo_map; from pathlib import Path; r = build_repo_map(Path('.'), budget_chars=200); print('Mapped:', r['files_mapped'], 'Omitted:', r['files_omitted'], 'Scanned:', r['files_scanned'], 'Truncated:', r['truncated'])".

what_it_checks: Whether lint_gate.check_file() gracefully handles missing ruff and returns checked=False (suggest-only, never blocks patches).
failure_symptom: Patch fails when ruff is not available (checked=True with error). Lint findings block patches or cause tool crashes.
likely_causes: lint_gate.py does not catch OSError/RuntimeError from subprocess call
patch_tool treats lint failures as fatal instead of advisory
Lint gate not wrapped with try-except in runner.patch_tool()
fix_steps: Verify lint_gate returns ok=True with checked=False when ruff is missing: check_file() should never raise
Check runner.patch_tool line ~130: lint gate is after syntax gate and only when result.success is True
Confirm patch succeeds even without lint: patch_tool should return ok=True regardless of lint findings
Test: disable lint with patch_tool(..., 'lint_gate': False) — patch should apply without invoking lint
If lint gate crashes, wrap it with try-except in runner.patch_tool or disable it per-call
change_robust: Test by making ruff unavailable and calling patch_tool. Verify result.ok is still True and lint key is present (or absent if disabled). Lint should never cause patch failure.
detection_method: Force ruff to be unavailable and lint a python file: python -c "import os; os.environ['CLUXION_SUPERCODER_RUFF_BIN'] = '/nonexistent'; from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core.lint_gate import check_file; from pathlib import Path; r = check_file(Path('x.py'), cwd=Path('.')); print('Checked:', r['checked'], 'Reason:', r.get('reason'))".

what_it_checks: Whether retry_loop state and repo_map outline cache are properly isolated when multiple agents or tasks run concurrently. These modules use in-process caches (OrderedDict) that are not thread-safe.
failure_symptom: Retry guidance is lost or wrong (attempt counter resets, wrong file's state used). Outline cache evicts wrong entries. In multi-agent setups, one agent's cursor invalidates another's.
likely_causes: retry_loop._failures dict is shared across threads without locks
repo_map._outline_cache is shared and not thread-safe for concurrent reads/writes
plugin.py registers tools without per-call isolation; all agents share plugin state
No workspace or task-id scoping in cache keys
fix_steps: Check if Hermes/plugin context is multi-threaded: review Hermes agent dispatch model (likely single-threaded per context)
If multi-threaded, add locks: import threading; _failures_lock = threading.Lock() in retry_loop.py
Test isolation: run two patch tasks in sequence (not parallel) and verify retry counters are correct
If concurrent agents share plugin process, reset retry_loop and repo_map state between tasks: call retry_loop.reset() and repo_map.clear_outline_cache()
Scope cache keys by task_id: prefix keys with (task_id, file_path) instead of just file_path
change_robust: Test by calling retry_loop functions from multiple threads (if Hermes is multi-threaded). Verify state is correct after concurrent calls. Check plugin invocation pattern in Hermes agent to understand threading model.
detection_method: Call retry_loop.record_failure() and retry_loop.record_success() concurrently on the same file, or clear outline_cache concurrently. Check that state is not corrupted: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.core import retry_loop, repo_map; retry_loop.reset(); import threading; def worker(): retry_loop.record_failure('ws', 'f.py', 'test'); threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker) for _ in range(5)]; [t.start() for t in threads]; [t.join() for t in threads]; print('Done')".

what_it_checks: Whether the plugin correctly registers 9 tools with Hermes via ctx.register_tool() in plugin.register(). Tools must have correct schemas, handlers, and emoji.
failure_symptom: Tools not available in Hermes CLI. Tool calls fail with 'unknown tool' error. Schema validation rejects arguments.
likely_causes: plugin.register() not called by Hermes entry point
Tool name mismatch between schema and runner function
Handler wrapping (_wrap) broken or missing
Schema JSON malformed or missing required fields
fix_steps: Verify plugin is registered: grep -r 'entry-points.*hermes_agent.plugins' pyproject.toml — should show cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder
Check registration: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.plugin import register; print('register function exists')"

Test handler wrapping: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.plugin import _wrap; from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder import runner; h = _wrap(runner.plan); print(h({'prompt': 'test', 'task_id': 't1'}))"
Validate schemas: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.schemas import *; import json; print(json.dumps(PLAN_SCHEMA, indent=2))"
Check Hermes loads plugin: cluxion-supercoder check — should return plugin=cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder
change_robust: Call register() with a mock Hermes context and verify all 9 tools are registered. Test each tool's handler via _wrap(). Validate schemas against JSON schema spec.
detection_method: Check plugin.py register() function: grep -c 'ctx.register_tool' src/cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder/plugin.py — should be exactly 9 (plan, read_window, patch, cursor_map, syntax_gate, lint_gate, repo_map, test_gate, brief). Verify schemas match runners: grep SCHEMA src/cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder/schemas.py | wc -l — should be 9.

what_it_checks: Whether tools receive and correctly resolve the workspace root (cwd parameter) from Hermes. All tools must respect the workspace boundary and never escape it.
failure_symptom: Tools read/write files outside the workspace (security breach). Path traversal attacks succeed. File operations leak data from parent directories.
likely_causes: read_window() or patch_tool() not validating that resolved path is_relative_to workspace root
safety.py _path_gate() not called for all file operations
Path resolution using string prefix instead of is_relative_to() (allows /work vs /work2 confusion)
fix_steps: Check read_window() in cursor.py line ~33: if not str(path).startswith(str(root)) — should use is_relative_to() instead
Verify safety.py _path_gate() uses is_relative_to(): grep -A2 'is_relative_to' src/cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder/core/safety.py
Test path escape: read_window({..., 'path': '../../../etc/passwd'}) — should raise PermissionError
Test symlink escape: if workspace has symlink to /tmp, verify resolved path is still within workspace
Confirm all file operations go through safety gate: patch_tool, read_window, cursor_map all validate paths
change_robust: Test path escape attempts: ../../../etc/passwd, /etc/passwd, symlinks to parent. Verify is_relative_to() is used. Check that workspace is resolved before comparison.
detection_method: Call tools with cwd parameter and verify path operations stay within bounds: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder import runner; from pathlib import Path; result = runner.read_window_tool({'cwd': '/tmp', 'path': '../../../etc/passwd', 'start_line': 1}); print('Error:', result.payload.get('error'))" — should raise PermissionError or return error, not read /etc/passwd.

what_it_checks: Whether all runner functions (via _wrap) return valid JSON even on error, and whether exceptions are caught and serialized correctly.
failure_symptom: Tool calls return invalid JSON or uncaught exceptions. Host agent crashes parsing response. Error messages are lost or truncated.
likely_causes: _wrap() does not catch all exceptions (only ValueError, FileNotFoundError, PermissionError)
ToolResult.to_json() fails due to non-serializable objects in payload
Exceptions raised in runner functions escape _wrap() without being caught
fix_steps: Check _wrap in plugin.py lines ~79-86: it catches (ValueError, FileNotFoundError, PermissionError) and returns json.dumps with ok=False

Test error path: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.plugin import _wrap; from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder import runner; h = _wrap(runner.read_window_tool); result = h({}); import json; j = json.loads(result); print('Error:', j.get('error'))"
Add more exception types if needed: OSError, UnicodeDecodeError, KeyError, etc.
Verify ToolResult.to_json() handles all payload types: avoid dicts with non-serializable values (Path, set, etc.)
Test with broken runner call: force an exception and verify json output is valid
change_robust: Call _wrap(runner.function) with invalid inputs. Verify output is valid JSON. Parse json.loads(result) and check 'ok' and 'error' keys.
detection_method: Call runner functions with invalid input and verify output is valid JSON: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder.plugin import _wrap; from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder import runner; h = _wrap(runner.read_window_tool); result = h({'cwd': '/nonexistent', 'path': 'x.py'}); import json; j = json.loads(result); print('ok' if 'ok' in j else 'invalid json')".

what_it_checks: Whether runtime dependencies (psutil, PyYAML, ruff) specified in pyproject.toml are installed. These are not optional.
failure_symptom: Import errors when plugin loads. psutil functions used in internal monitoring fail silently. YAML parsing (if used in config) crashes.
likely_causes: pip install skipped the 'install' extras
Requirements installed in wrong venv
Version mismatch (old ruff version before 0.8)
fix_steps: Check installed: pip show psutil PyYAML ruff
Reinstall plugin with deps: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-agentplugin-supercoder
Verify in correct venv: pip -V (should point to .hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/pip)
Check ruff version: ruff --version (should be >= 0.8 per pyproject.toml)
Test imports: python -c "import psutil, yaml; print('ok')"
change_robust: Import directly and catch ImportError. Use pip list to verify versions match pyproject.toml requirements.
detection_method: Try importing all: python -c "import psutil; import yaml; import ruff; print('all installed')" or pip list | grep -E '^(psutil|PyYAML|ruff)'.

what_it_checks: Whether patch_tool correctly blocks patches when stale_cursor=True is passed, or when expected_file_hash mismatches (file changed after read_window).
failure_symptom: Patches apply even when file changed after cursor was created. Silent data corruption due to stale cursor.
likely_causes: safety.pre_tool_gate() not checking stale_cursor parameter
stale_cursor parameter ignored or not passed through
fix_steps: Check safety.py line ~33: if stale_cursor: return SafetyDecision('block', ...)"
Verify patch_tool line ~91 calls pre_tool_gate with stale_cursor parameter
Test: call patch_tool with stale_cursor=True and verify result.ok is False
Test: call patch_tool with wrong expected_file_hash and verify stale_file error
change_robust: Test both stale_cursor=True (explicit flag) and hash mismatch (implicit stale detection). Verify both pathways return ok=False.
detection_method: Call patch_tool with stale_cursor=True and verify result.ok is False: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder import runner; r = runner.patch_tool({'cwd': '/tmp', 'path': 't.py', 'old_text': 'x', 'new_text': 'y', 'stale_cursor': True}); print('Blocked:', not r.ok, 'Reason:', r.payload.get('error'))".

what_it_checks: Whether safety._path_gate() blocks access to files matching secret patterns (.env, id_rsa, credentials, secrets).
failure_symptom: Patches apply to .env, credentials, secrets files. Sensitive data is modified. Secret keys are exposed in patch attempts.
likely_causes: safety._path_gate() not checking against _SECRET_PARTS tuple
Path matching uses basename only (allows .env in subdir that should be blocked)
fix_steps: Check safety.py line ~23: _SECRET_PARTS = ('.env', 'id_rsa', 'credentials', 'secrets')
Verify line ~56: if any(part in resolved.parts for part in _SECRET_PARTS) — this blocks any path component matching
Test: try patch_tool on .env, id_rsa, credentials.json, secrets.yaml — all should return blocked
Test subdirs: .config/.env, subdir/credentials.json — all should be blocked
If false positives (e.g., secretsmanager.py blocked), refine secret pattern matching
change_robust: Test with files matching secret patterns in various locations. Verify in resolved.parts (all path components) not just basename.
detection_method: Try to read/patch a .env file and verify it's blocked: python -c "from cluxion_agentplugin_supercoder import runner; from pathlib import Path; (Path('/tmp/.env').write_text('KEY=secret')); r = runner.read_window_tool({'cwd': '/tmp', 'path': '.env'}); print('Blocked:', not r.ok, 'Reason:', r.payload.get('error'))".
